# UC-22.6.13 · Cloud Shared Responsibility Control Coverage Map

## Description

Joins cloud configuration findings to a shared-responsibility matrix so use of cloud services shows which Annex controls are organization-operated.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes aws and sourcetypes aws:securityhub_finding. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Populate lookup from CSP matrix; (2) prioritize CRITICAL; (3) supplier review input. Indexes required: `index=aws`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:securityhub_finding`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk App for AWS Security Hub.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `aws:securityhub_finding`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Populate lookup from CSP matrix; (2) prioritize CRITICAL; (3) supplier review input.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=aws sourcetype=aws:securityhub_finding earliest=-7d RecordState=ACTIVE
| lookup csr_shared_responsibility.csv control_id OUTPUT annex_owner
| stats count by annex_owner, Severity.Label
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cloud Shared Responsibility Control Coverage Map (A.5.23)** — Joins cloud configuration findings to a shared-responsibility matrix so use of cloud services shows which Annex controls are organization-operated.

Documented **Data sources**: `aws:securityhub_finding`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk App for AWS Security Hub. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: aws; **sourcetype**: aws:securityhub_finding. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=aws, sourcetype=aws:securityhub_finding, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by annex_owner, Severity.Label** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap, Table

## SPL

```spl
index=aws sourcetype=aws:securityhub_finding earliest=-7d RecordState=ACTIVE
| lookup csr_shared_responsibility.csv control_id OUTPUT annex_owner
| stats count by annex_owner, Severity.Label
```

## Visualization

Heatmap, Table. Key fields: `Severity.Label`, `annex_owner`.
